step_name: Implement NIST AI RMF - Govern and Map

subprocess_flow.substep_name: Define AI Governance Roles and Responsibilities
subprocess_flow.description: This sub-step involves identifying and documenting the specific roles required for effective AI oversight within the organization. It includes defining the responsibilities of the AI Architect, the AI Ethics Committee, and other key stakeholders to ensure clear accountability and decision-making authority throughout the AI system's lifecycle.
success_criteria: Formal approval of the role matrix by the Head of AI Governance.
subprocess_flow.purpose: To establish clear accountability for AI risk management.
subprocess_flow.scope: Internal organizational roles related to the specific AI project.
end_conditions: Role matrix is documented and approved.

step_risks_and_controls.control: Detailed job descriptions and RACI matrix.

change_management.versioning_rules: Major version change for role additions.

subprocess_flow.description: In this sub-step, the team develops and formalizes the policies that will guide AI risk management activities. These policies must align with the NIST AI RMF 1.0 'Govern' core function and provide a structured approach for identifying, assessing, and managing risks specific to the financial sector's regulatory environment.
dependencies: Define AI Governance Roles and Responsibilities
success_criteria: Policy document is signed off by the Chief Compliance Officer.
subprocess_flow.purpose: To provide a standardized framework for risk-related decision making.
subprocess_flow.scope: Policy development for AI risk management.
triggers: Approval of AI Governance Role Matrix.
end_conditions: Policy is published in the internal repository.

subprocess_flow.substep_name: Map AI System Context and Boundaries
subprocess_flow.description: This sub-step focuses on defining the operational context of the AI system, including its intended purpose, target users, and the environment in which it will operate. By mapping these boundaries, the organization can better understand the potential impacts on individuals and society, which is a critical component of the NIST 'Map' function.
success_criteria: Context map accurately reflects the system's operational environment.
subprocess_flow.purpose: To identify the scope of potential risks based on system usage.
subprocess_flow.scope: Technical and operational boundaries of the AI system.
triggers: Publication of AI Risk Management Policy.
end_conditions: Context map is finalized and reviewed by technical leads.

step_risks_and_controls.control: Strict definition of system boundaries in the context map.

change_management.versioning_rules: Version tracking via model inventory system.

subprocess_flow.description: Building on the system context map, this sub-step involves identifying specific risks that could arise from the AI system's deployment. Risks are categorized according to their potential impact on organizational goals, data privacy, and regulatory compliance, ensuring that all relevant threats are captured in the project's risk register.
dependencies: Map AI System Context and Boundaries
success_criteria: Comprehensive list of risks identified and categorized.
subprocess_flow.purpose: To populate the risk register with context-specific threats.
triggers: Finalization of AI System Context Map.
end_conditions: Risk register is updated with initial mapping results.

change_management.versioning_rules: Incremental versioning for risk register updates.

subprocess_flow.substep_name: Consolidate NIST AI RMF Govern & Map Report
subprocess_flow.description: The final sub-step involves aggregating all findings from the governance and mapping activities into a single, comprehensive report. This document provides a clear overview of the established governance structures and the identified risk landscape, serving as the foundational artifact for subsequent measurement and management phases.
outputs: NIST AI RMF Govern & Map Report
success_criteria: Report is complete and meets all NIST AI RMF 1.0 documentation standards.
subprocess_flow.purpose: To provide a formal record of the Govern and Map phase results.
end_conditions: Report is signed off and distributed to stakeholders.

step_risks_and_controls.control: Peer review and sign-off by the Head of AI Governance.

change_management.versioning_rules: Major versioning for final report release.
